task_completion.description: Configure Bridge Command to broadcast NMEA data to localhost on port 10110
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the file '/home/ga/.config/Bridge Command/bc5.ini' with UDP address set to '127.0.0.1' and port set to '10110', OR the Bridge Command settings GUI displaying these network configuration values.

task_completion.description: Create the Python capture script on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE displaying the contents of 'capture_nmea.py' located on the Desktop. The code should show a UDP socket binding to port 10110, a print statement for incoming data, and file writing operations targeting '/home/ga/Documents/nmea_raw.log'.

task_completion.description: Run the Python script to capture and print NMEA data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution of 'capture_nmea.py' with NMEA sentences (e.g., strings starting with '$' like $GPGGA, $HEHDT) actively being printed to the standard output.

task_completion.description: Save raw NMEA sentences to the specified log file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal (using cat/less) or text editor showing the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/nmea_raw.log' populated with multiple raw NMEA sentences.

task_completion.description: Create the integration report with Talker IDs and GGA timestamp
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing '/home/ga/Documents/integration_report.txt'. The document must explicitly state the NMEA Talker IDs for Position and Heading, along with a UTC timestamp extracted from the first captured GGA sentence.

integrity.description: Verify the Python script was actually executed to capture data
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the script running and receiving data over time, rather than the agent manually echoing or pasting strings directly into the log file.

integrity.description: Verify the NMEA data appears to be genuinely generated by the simulator
integrity.visual_evidence: The NMEA sentences in the log file or terminal should have realistic formatting and varying timestamps (if visible over time), not just a single hardcoded string repeated.

integrity.description: Verify the report timestamp matches the captured logs
integrity.visual_evidence: The UTC timestamp mentioned in the integration report should correspond to the first GGA sentence visible in the captured 'nmea_raw.log' file, proving the agent extracted it from actual captured data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target IP: 127.0.0.1. Target Port: 10110. Script path: /home/ga/Desktop/capture_nmea.py. Log path: /home/ga/Documents/nmea_raw.log. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/integration_report.txt. Configuration file: /home/ga/.config/Bridge Command/bc5.ini. NMEA Talker IDs are the first two characters after the '$' (e.g., 'GP' or 'IN' for Position/GPS, 'HE' or 'IN' for Heading). GGA sentences contain the UTC timestamp in the first data field after the sentence formatter (e.g., in '$GPGGA,123519,...', the timestamp is 123519).
